answer_raw: 1. DECOMPOSE. FY2025 revenue by major offering was: Server products and cloud services $98.435 billion; Microsoft 365 Commercial products and cloud services $87.767 billion; Gaming $23.455 billion; LinkedIn $17.812 billion; Windows and Devices $17.314 billion; Search and news advertising $13.878 billion; Dynamics products and cloud services $7.827 billion; Enterprise and partner services $7.760 billion; and Microsoft 365 Consumer products and cloud services $7.404 billion (FY2025 10-K, Note 18 — Segment Information and Geographic Data). At the reporting-segment level, FY2025 revenue and operating income were $120.810 billion and $69.773 billion for Productivity and Business Processes, $106.265 billion and $44.589 billion for Intelligent Cloud, and $54.649 billion and $14.166 billion for More Personal Computing (FY2025 10-K, Note 18 — Segment Information and Geographic Data). 2. APPLY ECONOMIC FRAMEWORKS. Azure/cloud demand looks AI-expanded and capacity-bound: Azure and other cloud services grew 34% in FY2025, and Q2 FY2026 Azure and other cloud services grew 39% in constant currency while demand exceeded supply (FY2025 10-K, Item 7, Intelligent Cloud; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks). Complementarity is strong because management described agents as requiring model catalogs, tuning, orchestration, context engineering, AI safety, management, observability, and security; Foundry customers spending $1 million-plus per quarter grew nearly 80%, over 250 customers were on track to process over 1 trillion tokens, and Fabric exceeded a $2 billion annualized revenue run rate with over 31,000 customers and 60% revenue growth (Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Satya Nadella prepared remarks). The supply constraint is both a moat and a risk: Microsoft added nearly one gigawatt of capacity in Q2 FY2026, capital expenditures were $37.5 billion with roughly two-thirds on short-lived GPUs and CPUs, and Amy Hood said many large GPU contracts are sold for the useful life of the GPUs; however, FY2025 purchase commitments were $109.953 billion and operating and finance lease obligations including imputed interest were $178.701 billion, indicating high capital intensity (Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Satya Nadella prepared remarks; Amy Hood prepared remarks and Q&A with Mark Moerdler; FY2025 10-K, Item 7, Contractual Obligations). Microsoft 365 Commercial is an application-layer AI monetization unit: FY2025 Microsoft 365 Commercial products and cloud services grew 14% and Microsoft 365 Commercial cloud grew 15%, while Q2 FY2026 Microsoft 365 commercial cloud grew 17% in constant currency, seats exceeded 450 million, and Microsoft 365 Copilot reached 15 million paid seats with paid seat adds up over 160% (FY2025 10-K, Item 7, Productivity and Business Processes; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks; Satya Nadella prepared remarks). Assumption: if Copilot continues to raise ARPU faster than AI serving costs, Microsoft 365 can compound revenue with high customer embeddedness; counter-risk is that the 10-K identifies competition from software/global application vendors, web-based and mobile application companies, AI-first application companies, and local developers (FY2025 10-K, Item 1, Productivity and Business Processes — Competition). LinkedIn and Dynamics are smaller but AI-complementary: FY2025 LinkedIn revenue grew 9%, Dynamics products and cloud services grew 15%, Dynamics 365 grew 19%, and Q2 FY2026 Dynamics 365 grew 19% in constant currency; however, LinkedIn competes with professional networks, recruiting, talent-management, advertising, and lead-generation outlets, while Dynamics competes with business-solution providers (FY2025 10-K, Item 7, Productivity and Business Processes; FY2025 10-K, Item 1, Competition; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks). Server products are mixed: FY2025 Server products revenue declined 3% because of customer shift to cloud offerings, but Q2 FY2026 on-premises server revenue rose 21% in constant currency on hybrid solutions, SQL Server 2025, and transactional purchasing ahead of memory-price increases (FY2025 10-K, Item 7, Intelligent Cloud; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks). In More Personal Computing, Search can benefit from AI distribution but faces substitution and execution risk: FY2025 Search and news advertising ex-TAC grew 20%, Copilot daily users nearly tripled in Q2 FY2026, but Q2 FY2026 search ex-TAC grew 10% in constant currency and was slightly below expectations because of execution challenges (FY2025 10-K, Item 7, More Personal Computing; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Satya Nadella prepared remarks; Amy Hood prepared remarks). Windows and Devices are tied to PC cycles with AI-PC optionality: FY2025 Windows and Devices revenue was $17.314 billion, Windows OEM and Devices grew 3%, Q2 FY2026 Windows 11 users surpassed 1 billion and were up over 45%, but management warned memory pricing could create additional volatility in Windows OEM and server transactional purchasing (FY2025 10-K, Note 18 — Segment Information and Geographic Data; FY2025 10-K, Item 7, More Personal Computing; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Satya Nadella prepared remarks; Amy Hood prepared remarks). Gaming is the weakest AI-diffusion fit in the disclosures: FY2025 Gaming revenue grew 9% and Xbox content and services grew 16%, but Q2 FY2026 Gaming revenue fell 9% in constant currency and Xbox content and services fell 5% in constant currency, with impairment charges in gaming (FY2025 10-K, Item 7, More Personal Computing; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks). 3. SYNTHESIZE. My thesis is bullish on long-term revenue but mixed on margins: AI diffusion should expand Azure consumption and raise Microsoft 365, Dynamics, GitHub, and security monetization, but the margin path depends on whether utilization, custom silicon, and software efficiency keep pace with depreciation, leases, and power/capacity costs. Evidence for the upside is that Microsoft Cloud revenue reached $168.9 billion in FY2025, up 23%, and then reached $51.5 billion in Q2 FY2026, up 26% in constant currency (FY2025 10-K, Item 7, Overview; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks). Evidence for the margin risk is that FY2025 Microsoft Cloud gross margin was 69%, Q2 FY2026 company gross margin was 68% and Microsoft Cloud gross margin was 67%, and Q3 FY2026 cloud gross margin guidance was roughly 65%, all pressured by AI infrastructure investment and usage (FY2025 10-K, Item 7, Summary Results of Operations; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks). Assumption: if capacity remains scarce and contracted for long-duration demand, infrastructure scale becomes a moat; if utilization or pricing disappoints, the same capacity commitments become a ROIC risk.

[FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 7, Uncertain Tax Positions; Note 11—Income Taxes; Part I, Item 1A, Operational Risks; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks] Q2 FY2026 confirms both momentum and the investment burden: total revenue was $81.3 billion, up 17% in constant currency; Microsoft Cloud revenue was $51.5 billion, up 26% in constant currency; operating income increased 21% in constant currency; but company gross margin was 68%, Microsoft Cloud gross margin was 67%, and capex was $37.5 billion, with roughly two-thirds on short-lived GPUs and CPUs. [Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks] Overall, Microsoft is financially healthy because profitability, operating cash flow, liquidity, and segment breadth are strong, but its risk profile is shifting toward AI-infrastructure ROIC, supply-chain execution, OpenAI exposure, regulatory scrutiny, and tax uncertainty.

answer_raw: Productivity and Business Processes is the strongest profitability engine. FY2025 segment revenue was $120.810 billion, up 13%, and operating income was $69.773 billion, up 17%. [FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 7, Segment Results of Operations; Note 18—Segment Information and Geographic Data] Growth was driven by Microsoft 365 Commercial products and cloud services revenue up 14%, Microsoft 365 Commercial cloud revenue up 15%, Microsoft 365 Consumer products and cloud services revenue up 11%, LinkedIn revenue up 9%, Dynamics products and cloud services revenue up 15%, and Dynamics 365 revenue up 19%. [FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 7, Productivity and Business Processes] Management's Q2 FY2026 commentary reinforced the segment's AI monetization: Productivity and Business Processes revenue was $34.1 billion, up 16% in constant currency; Microsoft 365 Commercial cloud revenue grew 17% in constant currency; paid Microsoft 365 Commercial seats grew 6% to over 450 million; Microsoft 365 Copilot had 15 million paid seats; and Productivity and Business Processes operating margin was 60%. [Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood and Satya Nadella prepared remarks] Strategic priority in this segment is to monetize AI through Copilot, WorkIQ, agents, E5, Dynamics agents, and LinkedIn AI-enabled engagement, while the key headwind is that segment gross margin percentage decreased slightly in FY2025 primarily from scaling AI infrastructure, partly offset by efficiency gains. [FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 7, Productivity and Business Processes; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Satya Nadella prepared remarks] Intelligent Cloud is the principal infrastructure growth engine but also the center of capital intensity. FY2025 Intelligent Cloud revenue was $106.265 billion, up 21%, and operating income was $44.589 billion, up 18%; Server products and cloud services revenue increased 23%, Azure and other cloud services revenue grew 34%, and Server products revenue decreased 3%. [FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 7, Intelligent Cloud; Note 18—Segment Information and Geographic Data] In Q2 FY2026, Intelligent Cloud revenue was $32.9 billion, up 29% in constant currency; Azure and other cloud services revenue grew 39% in constant currency; on-premises server revenue increased 21% in constant currency; and operating margin was 42%. [Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks] Strategic priorities are Azure capacity expansion, the AI token factory, Foundry, Fabric, model choice, custom silicon, and agent deployment: Satya Nadella said Microsoft added nearly 1 gigawatt of total capacity in the quarter, Fabric annualized revenue run rate exceeded $2 billion, Foundry customers spending $1 million-plus per quarter grew nearly 80%, and Maia 200 delivers over 30% improved TCO compared with the latest generation hardware in Microsoft's fleet. [Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Satya Nadella prepared remarks] Headwinds are supply and margins: FY2025 Intelligent Cloud gross margin percentage decreased due to scaling AI infrastructure, Q2 FY2026 demand continued to exceed supply, and management highlighted continued investments in AI as a gross-margin pressure. [FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 7, Intelligent Cloud; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks] More Personal Computing is strategically valuable but more uneven. FY2025 segment revenue was $54.649 billion, up 7%, and operating income was $14.166 billion, up 18%; Windows and Devices revenue increased 2%, Gaming revenue increased 9%, Xbox content and services revenue increased 16%, Xbox hardware revenue decreased 25%, Search and news advertising revenue increased 13%, and Search and news advertising ex-TAC increased 20%. [FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 7, More Personal Computing; Note 18—Segment Information and Geographic Data] Q2 FY2026 showed the headwinds: More Personal Computing revenue was $14.3 billion, down 3%; Gaming revenue decreased 9% in constant currency; Xbox content and services decreased 5% in constant currency and was below expectations; Windows OEM grew 5%; and Search and news advertising ex-TAC increased 10% in constant currency but was slightly below expectations. [Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks] Strategic priorities are Windows 11 adoption, Copilot consumer experiences, Bing and Edge share gains, and gaming across Xbox, PC, cloud, and other devices; Satya said Windows 11 reached 1 billion users, up over 45% year over year, while gaming saw record PC players and paid streaming hours. [Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Satya Nadella prepared remarks] My view is that Productivity and Business Processes and Intelligent Cloud are the durable AI-growth core, while More Personal Computing is a portfolio of options with stronger search and Windows AI upside but near-term gaming and hardware volatility.

answer_raw: DECOMPOSE — Microsoft's FY2025 revenue base was $281.724 billion, with major product/service units led by Server products and cloud services at $98.435 billion, Microsoft 365 Commercial products and cloud services at $87.767 billion, Gaming at $23.455 billion, LinkedIn at $17.812 billion, Windows and Devices at $17.314 billion, Search and news advertising at $13.878 billion, Dynamics products and cloud services at $7.827 billion, Enterprise and partner services at $7.760 billion, and Microsoft 365 Consumer products and cloud services at $7.404 billion. [FY2025 10-K, Note 18—Segment Information and Geographic Data, Revenue by significant product and service offerings] At the reporting-segment level, Productivity and Business Processes generated $120.810 billion of revenue and $69.773 billion of operating income, Intelligent Cloud generated $106.265 billion of revenue and $44.589 billion of operating income, and More Personal Computing generated $54.649 billion of revenue and $14.166 billion of operating income. [FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 7, Segment Results of Operations; Note 18—Segment Information and Geographic Data] APPLY — Microsoft 365 Commercial is the clearest application-layer AI monetization unit: FY2025 Microsoft 365 Commercial products and cloud services revenue increased 14%, driven by Microsoft 365 Commercial cloud growth of 15%, and Q2 FY2026 Microsoft 365 Commercial cloud revenue grew 17% in constant currency while paid seats grew 6% to over 450 million. [FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 7, Productivity and Business Processes; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks] Demand and complementarity are positive because management said ARPU growth was led by E5 and Microsoft 365 Copilot, Microsoft 365 Copilot had 15 million paid seats, seat adds were up over 160% year over year, and the number of customers with over 35,000 seats tripled year over year. [Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood and Satya Nadella prepared remarks] Substitution risk exists because the 10-K states Office competes with software, global application vendors, web-based and mobile application companies, AI-first application companies, and local application developers; however, Microsoft's integration of Office, Windows, Copilot, and Enterprise Mobility + Security creates a bundle advantage if AI value accrues through workflow integration. [FY2025 10-K, Part I, Item 1, Productivity and Business Processes—Competition; Part I, Item 1, Microsoft 365 Commercial Products and Cloud Services] Supply constraints matter even for Microsoft 365 because FY2025 segment gross margin percentage decreased slightly, primarily from scaling AI infrastructure, partly offset by efficiency gains in Microsoft 365 Commercial cloud. [FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 7, Productivity and Business Processes] LinkedIn is a smaller but AI-enhanceable marketplace and data asset: FY2025 LinkedIn revenue increased 9%, and Q2 FY2026 LinkedIn revenue increased 11% in constant currency, led by Marketing Solutions. [FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 7, Productivity and Business Processes; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks] Dynamics is another application-layer beneficiary: FY2025 Dynamics products and cloud services revenue increased 15%, Dynamics 365 revenue grew 19%, and Q2 FY2026 Dynamics 365 revenue increased 19% in constant currency; demand should benefit if AI agents automate sales, service, finance, and operations workflows. [FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 7, Productivity and Business Processes; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood and Satya Nadella prepared remarks] Azure is the core infrastructure and platform beneficiary: FY2025 Server products and cloud services revenue increased 23% and Azure and other cloud services revenue grew 34%, while Q2 FY2026 Intelligent Cloud revenue was $32.9 billion, up 29% in constant currency, and Azure and other cloud services grew 39% in constant currency. [FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 7, Intelligent Cloud; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks] Demand and complementarity are strong because Satya Nadella described agents as the new apps, Foundry customers spending $1 million-plus per quarter grew nearly 80%, Fabric annualized revenue run rate exceeded $2 billion with over 31,000 customers and revenue up 60% year over year, and over 250 customers are on track to process over 1 trillion tokens on Foundry this year. [Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Satya Nadella prepared remarks] Substitution risk is real because the 10-K states Azure competes with cloud service providers and open-source offerings, and AI offerings compete with hyperscalers, emerging competitors, and open-source offerings. [FY2025 10-K, Part I, Item 1, Intelligent Cloud—Competition] Supply constraints are both moat and risk: the 10-K states datacenters depend on permitted and buildable land, predictable energy, networking supplies, and servers including GPUs; Q2 FY2026 capex was $37.5 billion, roughly two-thirds on short-lived assets such as GPUs and CPUs; Satya said Microsoft added nearly 1 gigawatt of capacity in the quarter; and Amy said customer demand continues to exceed supply. [FY2025 10-K, Part I, Item 1, Operations; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Satya Nadella and Amy Hood prepared remarks] Server products are more mixed: FY2025 Server products revenue decreased 3% due to lower transactional purchasing and cloud shift, but Q2 FY2026 on-premises server revenue increased 21% in constant currency due to hybrid solutions and SQL Server 2025, implying AI diffusion can pull workloads to cloud while hybrid requirements preserve some on-prem demand. [FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 7, Intelligent Cloud; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks] Windows and Devices are moderate beneficiaries but PC-cycle exposed: FY2025 Windows and Devices revenue increased 2%, Windows OEM and Devices revenue increased 3%, Q2 FY2026 Windows OEM grew 5%, and Satya said Windows 11 reached 1 billion users, up over 45% year over year. [FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 7, More Personal Computing; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood and Satya Nadella prepared remarks] Gaming is mixed-to-negative near term: FY2025 Gaming revenue increased 9% and Xbox content and services revenue increased 16%, but Xbox hardware revenue decreased 25%; Q2 FY2026 Gaming revenue decreased 9% in constant currency and Xbox content and services decreased 5% in constant currency, below expectations. [FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 7, More Personal Computing; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks] Search and news advertising is a high-optionality but substitution-exposed AI unit: FY2025 Search and news advertising revenue increased 13% and ex-TAC growth was 20%, while Q2 FY2026 ex-TAC growth was 10% in constant currency and below expectations; the 10-K states Search and news advertising competes with search engines, websites, social platforms, and portals, so AI chat can be either traffic driver or substitute. [FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 7, More Personal Computing; Part I, Item 1, More Personal Computing—Competition; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks] SYNTHESIZE — My base case is mixed bullish: assuming AI value accrues primarily to integrated platforms rather than stand-alone tools, Microsoft should compound revenue through Azure consumption, Microsoft 365 Copilot ARPU, Dynamics agents, GitHub Copilot, security, and data platforms, with FY2025 Microsoft Cloud revenue already at $168.9 billion, up 23%, and Q2 FY2026 Microsoft Cloud revenue at $51.5 billion, up 26% in constant currency. [FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 7, Overview; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Satya Nadella and Amy Hood prepared remarks] Margin trajectory is likely uneven: FY2025 Microsoft Cloud gross margin declined to 69%, Q2 FY2026 Microsoft Cloud gross margin was 67%, and Q3 FY2026 cloud gross margin guidance was roughly 65%, all reflecting AI infrastructure investment; however, FY2025 operating income still grew 17% versus revenue growth of 15%, and management now expects FY2026 operating margins to be up slightly. [FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 7, Summary Results of Operations; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks] The bear case is that AI diffusion is capital-intensive and concentrated: FY2025 additions to property and equipment were $64.551 billion, FY2025 purchase commitments were $109.953 billion, additional leases not yet commenced were $92.7 billion, and Q2 FY2026 commercial RPO was $625 billion with approximately 45% from OpenAI, creating execution and customer-concentration sensitivity. [FY2025 10-K, Part II, Item 8, Cash Flows Statements; Part II, Item 7, Contractual Obligations; Note 13—Leases; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks] The bull case is that capacity is pre-sold and strategically scarce: Amy said much of the GPU capital being bought is already contracted for most of its useful life, while Satya emphasized optimizing tokens per watt per dollar and cited a 50% throughput improvement in OpenAI inferencing and over 30% better TCO for Maia 200 versus the latest fleet hardware. [Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood Q&A with Mark Moerdler; Satya Nadella prepared remarks] Overall, I would expect Microsoft's long-term revenue growth to be led by Intelligent Cloud and Productivity and Business Processes, while consolidated gross margins remain pressured by AI infrastructure, and operating margins depend on utilization, pricing power in Copilot and Azure, and whether agentic demand scales faster than capex depreciation and lease costs.
